pr.title: gr/gm: decompile stage and game functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled seven previously placeholder functions across adventure-mode game logic and stage code for Hyrule Castle and Onett. The PR also tightened several headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures and refined local unknown struct fields needed by the matches. Verification evidence is strong: the PR body says all functions matched 100% via objdiff and main.dol SHA1 was OK, and the decomp-dev bot reported +1072 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Decompiled fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, gm_80180B18, and gm_80181A44; refined local unknown structs/globals used by adventure-mode logic.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes for fn_8017E318, gm_80180B18, and gm_80181A44 with concrete int/void signatures.
Decompiled grCastle_801CE860 and fn_801D0924; added cm/camera.h for Camera_80030E44.
Typed fn_801D0924 as void fn_801D0924(HSD_GObj*, int).
Decompiled grOnett_801E3CE4, an Onett ground initialization/callback setup routine.
Typed grOnett_801E40E4 as a Ground/CollData/mpLib_GroundEnum callback signature.

what_changed: Implemented fn_8017E318 as a 12-entry accumulator over lbl_804706D8, summing x2 for entries whose x0 is not -1.

Implemented gm_8017E7FC with adventure-mode branching based on gm_801A4310() == 4, cpu_level >= 2, and an unsigned threshold check against 0xFD20U; it sets lbl_80472C30.x77, may set StartMeleeRules.x4_5, and queues minor scene 0x5A.
Implemented gm_80180B18 as a loop over 0x1B entries, using gm_80164024 and gmMainLib_8015D06C to cache scaled values into lbl_80472E48.x14.
Implemented gm_80181A44 as a switch over arg1 values 0x21 through 0x26, writing a bool byte for c_kind into lbl_803D8D08 table bands at offsets 0x6C, 0x12C, 0x1EC, 0x2AC, 0x36C, and 0x42C.

Implemented grCastle_801CE860 to update Hyrule Castle animation state/timer fields gv.castle.xC4 and xC8, call grAnime_801C8138 when the timer expires, load the next s16 timer through grCs_804D6970 pointer arithmetic, and call Camera_80030E44.
Implemented fn_801D0924 to call grDisplay_801C5DB0 and, when gp->x10_flags.b7 is set, render up to 12 castle3 entries with local GXColor values through lb_800117F4.
Implemented grOnett_801E3CE4 to initialize Onett ground/JOBJ animation state, clear callbacks, set x10_flags.b5, and register grOnett_801E40E4 with mpJointSetCb1 for joints 3 and 4.
Refined gmregclear local struct layout by replacing padding with concrete fields such as lbl_80472D28_t.x108, x118, x11A, and x11B, while keeping unknown globals named by address.
Updated headers so newly decompiled routines expose concrete signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Used narrow field types such as s16 and u8 in partially known structs to match the observed loads/stores without over-naming behavior that the evidence does not prove.
Kept address-based names for still-unknown functions/globals while improving signatures, preserving project searchability and avoiding invented semantic names.
Mirrored the nearby gm_80180BA0 save/restore-style loop when writing gm_80180B18, including gm_80164024-based indexing and /10 versus *0xA scaling.
Introduced the real camera include for grCastle_801CE860 instead of relying on placeholder declarations.
Preserved stack-shape helpers where needed: PAD_STACK(4) in the Castle render helper and PAD_STACK(8) in the Onett initialization helper.
Updated callback typing in gronett.h after grOnett_801E3CE4 registered grOnett_801E40E4 with mpJointSetCb1, making later stage-collision work easier.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling game-mode table helpers, adjacent inverse routines can reveal scaling and indexing conventions; here gm_80180B18 and gm_80180BA0 form a useful /10 and *0xA pair around lbl_80472E48.x14.
Do not remove apparently redundant calls too aggressively: the diff keeps gm_80164024((u8) i) in places even when idx exists, which is often necessary for exact codegen.
For switch-based table writers, record the exact byte offsets and stride before inventing a struct; gm_80181A44 exposes repeated lbl_803D8D08 bands spaced by 0xC0.
Stage render callbacks may require local GXColor temporaries and explicit PAD_STACK to match, even when the logic is straightforward.
Stage init functions often combine Ground/JOBJ setup, animation start, gv union field initialization, callback clearing, flag setting, and mpJoint callback registration in one routine.
Header cleanup is part of a match: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete returns and arguments documents the discovered ABI and helps downstream callsites.
naming_conventions: Module prefixes were preserved: gm_ for game-mode routines, grCastle_ for Hyrule Castle stage routines, and grOnett_ for Onett stage routines.
Unidentified local functions retained address-style fn_ names, such as fn_8017E318 and fn_801D0924.
Unknown globals retained lbl_ address names such as lbl_804706D8, lbl_80472C30, lbl_80472E48, and lbl_803D8D08.
Partially known struct fields use xOFFSET names such as x108, x118, x11A, x11B, xC4, and xC8, with pad_* retained only where layout remains unknown.
Callback signatures should be typed once known; grOnett_801E40E4 became void grOnett_801E40E4(Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32).
Character-kind table helpers use the existing c_kind parameter name when indexing per-character byte tables.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit u8 casts for loop indices passed to gm_80164024.
Used a bool temporary cond and an unsigned compare against 0xFD20U in gm_8017E7FC to preserve branch structure.
Used u8* pointer arithmetic plus an s16 load at grCs_804D6970 + xC4 * 2 + 0x12C for the Castle animation timer.
Used local GXColor initializers with alpha 0xFE and 0xFF in fn_801D0924.
Used PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(8) to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used GET_GROUND and GET_JOBJ macros in the Onett initializer while Castle code accessed gobj->user_data directly, matching local code style and generated code needs.
Verification cited both objdiff 100% matches and main.dol SHA1 OK.

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported all functions at 100% match via objdiff and main.dol: OK SHA1 verification.
Reported 7 new matches and +1072 matched bytes, with matched code moving to 57.72% (+0.03%).
Listed grOnett_801E3CE4, fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, fn_801D0924, gm_80180B18, gm_80181A44, and grCastle_801CE860 as moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
Also reported a grcastle .sdata2 improvement of +9 bytes, 48.08% to 51.88%.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
